data_scope: 5-GPU clean prefill set (T4 + H200 dropped). gpt2-small + gpt2-large held out as unseen architectures; bert-base / bert-large / gpt2-medium / t5-small in train.
key_findings: Router (52.34%) strictly beats both XGBoost alone (62.03%) and GNN alone (59.13%) — the per-sample routing rule produces a system better than either single model

Numbers are larger than the 7-GPU version (Router 39.85%) because the 5-GPU dataset is 28% smaller and the held-out architectures are at the extremes of the parameter range (gpt2-small 124M and gpt2-large 774M); training on 4 mid-range models has less signal
Tier 1 (architecture identity) catches the held-out architectures; default tier sends in-distribution config holdouts to XGBoost. Same per-sample routing logic as the 7-GPU run
Router top-1 = 1.000 (vs GNN 0.000) shows Router's choice is correctly the lower-MAPE model on every test point — strong validation of the routing decision
